findings.title: The new concurrency-limit test still relies on sleep-based synchronization
findings.why: The added coverage for `getReadRangeFromPropsConcurrency` infers that the third open cannot start by sleeping for a fixed 100ms window after the first two opens begin. That makes the assertion depend on scheduler timing instead of an explicit happens-before signal.
findings.risk_if_unchanged: This regression test can miss real limit violations on slow or contended CI runners, or fail intermittently when goroutine scheduling does not line up with the fixed sleep window.
findings.evidence: After the test waits for two `startedCh` notifications, it calls `time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)` and only then asserts `store.max.Load() == 2`. There is still no explicit synchronization proving that a third `Open` could not start just after the sleep or that the blocked path was exercised before the assertion.
findings.change_request: Replace the fixed sleep with an explicit barrier or channel-based assertion that proves the third open cannot begin before the first two readers are released, then close the barrier and verify the helper completes.
